mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or the signal's rank correlation drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the threshold effect loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real auto sales and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across historical expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which consistently produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or the signal's rank correlation drops below 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310, accelerating inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches 310, or inflation expectations fail to rise alongside the index.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicates synchronized labor and output expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 40% across historical downturn periods.
